mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth rate shows no significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following the specified UNRATE trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive on average during periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are elevated according to the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing and supply chain activity, signaling healthy corporate performance and driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth fails to predict positive S&P 500 5-day returns during confirmed periods of economic expansion over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a weakening construction sector and broader economic demand, which can signal an impending economic slowdown and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls > 10% from prior 6-month average
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances following a significant HOUST decline trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer confidence, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in PAYEMS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden and significant cut in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) by the central bank often signals an attempt to stimulate a weakening economy, which can initially be perceived negatively by markets due to underlying economic concerns, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances following a FEDFUNDS rate cut trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong, but the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is also low, it indicates a robust and potentially overheating economy, which could lead to inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, eventually dampening equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive during periods of high GDP growth and low unemployment as defined by the trigger.
